pattern: When one value fills half of a 2n array, two copies must land within a short window, so a tiny look-back or a single seen-set find it in one pass.
hints: Half of all the slots hold the same value, so collisions are extremely common.
Track which values you have already encountered; the first repeat you find is the answer.
Scan once, adding each value to a set; return the first value that is already present.

edge_inputs.description: smallest array, repeat at the end
repeat is interleaved through the array
repeated value appears at every other index
two equal values are far apart
repeated value clustered at the front

stress.description: build a valid 2n array with n unique singletons plus one value repeated n times, then shuffle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size // 2)
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 10001), n + 1)
    rep = vals[0]
    singles = vals[1:]
    arr = singles + [rep] * n
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr]
stress.note: exactly n+1 unique values, one repeated n times, as the problem guarantees
